food_subgroup: Filipino silog breakfast plate (cured/marinated meat, garlic fried rice, fried egg)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Tapsilog. FOODON_00002473 (beef dish) is the closest defensible parent. A more specific Filipino silog term does not exist in the current FoodOn release.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Tapsilog as a composite dish. A0CKP (Beef, cooked) is used as the closest parent anchor for the primary protein component. The composite nature (garlic rice, fried egg) is not captured by a single FoodEx2 code.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept exists for Tapsilog or Filipino silog meals. No suitable parent concept identified without risk of fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Tapsilog is approximately 350 g, comprising roughly 100 g of pan-fried beef tapa, 200 g of garlic fried rice (sinangag), and one fried egg (~50 g).

nutrient_source.title: Tapsilog — composite nutrient estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central component foods: Beef, cured/marinated, cooked (FDC ID 174033); White rice, cooked, with fat added (FDC ID 168878); Egg, whole, fried (FDC ID 173424); Garlic, raw (FDC ID 169230)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA or HPB entry exists for Tapsilog as a composite dish. Nutrient values per 100 g are estimated by weighted-average composition of the three primary components at their standard serving proportions: beef tapa 100 g (29%), garlic fried rice 200 g (57%), fried egg 50 g (14%). Beef tapa values adapted from USDA SR Legacy 'Beef, cured, dried' and 'Beef, short loin, cooked, pan-fried' entries; garlic fried rice from USDA 'Rice, white, cooked' with oil adjustment; fried egg from USDA FDC 173424. Sodium elevated to reflect soy sauce and salt used in marinade. This is a medium-confidence composite estimate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white rice, cooked (GI ~64–72); composite meal GI estimated at ~56 due to protein and fat co-ingestion attenuating glycemic response
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021. Am J Clin Nutr. 2021.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Tapsilog as a composite dish. GI estimated at 56 (medium) based on the primary carbohydrate source being white jasmine-style garlic fried rice (GI ~64 for plain white rice per University of Sydney GI Database), attenuated downward by the substantial protein (beef tapa ~10.5 g/100g) and fat (7.2 g/100g) content of the composite meal, consistent with published mixed-meal GI attenuation literature. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI 56 × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (56 × 78.75 g carbohydrate in 350 g serving) / 100 = 44.1.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg/100 g; ~1470 mg per standard 350 g serving) from soy sauce marinade and added salt
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Tapsilog to occasional consumption and request reduced-soy-sauce preparation, as a single serving can contribute approximately 64% of the WHO-recommended 2300 mg daily sodium limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (~56) driven by white garlic fried rice as the primary carbohydrate source, partially offset by protein and fat co-ingestion
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size carefully, as the 350 g serving delivers approximately 78.75 g of carbohydrate with a glycemic load of ~44, which is high; substituting brown rice or reducing rice portion can meaningfully lower the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~2.1 g/100 g) and elevated sodium from marinade; cholesterol contribution from fried egg (~95 mg/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, Tapsilog should be consumed in moderation; choosing leaner beef cuts, reducing cooking oil, and limiting soy sauce in the marinade can reduce saturated fat and sodium load per serving.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tapsilog is a quintessential Filipino breakfast and all-day meal consisting of three components encoded in its portmanteau name: 'tap' from tapa (thin slices of beef marinated in soy sauce, vinegar, garlic, sugar, and black pepper, then pan-fried or grilled until slightly caramelised), 'si' from sinangag (garlic fried rice made by stir-frying day-old cooked white rice with generous amounts of minced garlic in oil until fragrant and lightly crisp), and 'log' from itlog (a fried egg, typically sunny-side up). The dish is served as a single plate with all three components arranged together, often accompanied by a dipping sauce of vinegar with garlic and chilli. Texturally, Tapsilog offers a contrast between the chewy, savoury-sweet beef, the slightly crispy and aromatic garlic rice, and the soft, runny egg yolk. It is a staple of Filipino carinderias (local eateries), fast-food chains such as Jollibee and Max's, and Filipino restaurants worldwide including in Singapore. Nutritionally, a standard 350 g serving provides approximately 683 kcal, 36.75 g protein, 25.2 g fat, and 78.75 g carbohydrate. The dish is calorie-dense and sodium-rich due to the soy sauce marinade (~1470 mg sodium per serving). The medium glycemic index (~56) is driven by white rice but partially attenuated by protein and fat. Clinically, Tapsilog warrants caution for individuals managing hypertension (high sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (high glycemic load per serving), and cardiovascular disease (saturated fat and cholesterol from egg and beef). It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those avoiding soy allergens. Halal status depends on the beef source and preparation method.

confidence_reason: No single approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA) contains a direct Tapsilog composite entry. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted-average composition of USDA FoodData Central component foods (beef tapa, garlic fried rice, fried egg) at standard serving proportions. GI is an analogous estimate based on white rice GI from the University of Sydney GI Database with mixed-meal attenuation. Medium confidence is appropriate for a well-characterised composite dish estimated from approved component sources.
notes: 1. Tapsilog is a composite Filipino dish; no single approved database entry exists. All per_100g values are weighted-average estimates from USDA FoodData Central component entries as documented in nutrient_source. 2. Sodium value (~420 mg/100g) is elevated above plain rice/beef/egg baselines to reflect the soy sauce and salt used in the tapa marinade; this is a conservative estimate and actual restaurant sodium may be higher. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA component data and carry medium confidence; no -1 sentinel values are used as reasonable estimates are available from approved sources. 4. Halal status is marked false as beef tapa preparation and sourcing cannot be confirmed halal without restaurant-specific certification. 5. The food_group has been reclassified from the input 'Grains and staples' to 'Mixed' as Tapsilog is a composite plate of protein, starch, and egg. 6. GI glycemic_load_per_serving = (56 × 78.75) / 100 = 44.1, calculated for the 350 g standard serving containing approximately 78.75 g carbohydrate. 7. FoodOn URI FOODON_00002473 is used as closest_parent (beef dish); no exact FoodOn term for Tapsilog exists. FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Beef, cooked) is used as closest_parent for the primary protein component.
